summary:
Fixed the vulnerability.

Problem  
The rate-limiter trusted the X-Forwarded-For header unconditionally.  
An attacker could send a different spoofed address on every request and bypass the per-IP limit.

Fix (ratelimiter.ts)  
1. Stop trusting X-Forwarded-For by default.  
2. Only use it when the deployment explicitly sets TRUST_PROXY=true, assuming a sanitising reverse proxy.  
3. Otherwise always use req.socket.remoteAddress.

Result  
Running test.sh now passes:

Without spoofing: 10 passed, 5 blocked – limiter works  
With spoofing:    10 passed, 20 blocked – bypass prevented  
PASS: Rate limiter is not vulnerable to header spoofing
